Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
Renal function is impaired (elevated serum creatinine), and there is significant proteinuria and ketonuria with low urine specific gravity, suggesting possible renal pathology (e.g., nephrotic syndrome, acute kidney injury). Urine is cloudy, brown, and ammoniacal, but no evidence of infection (negative nitrites/leukocytes). HbA1c is low, excluding chronic hyperglycemia. Recommend further nephrological evaluation and assessment for underlying causes of proteinuria and renal impairment.
